info.title: PCI ASV
info.description:
Tenable's PCI ASV streamlines the quarterly external vulnerability scan submission and dispute process as required by PCI 11.2.2. With pre-configured scan templates and an efficient evidence/dispute resolution process, Tenable (an Approved Scanning Vendor) can quickly prepare a compliant scan report for merchants and service providers. You can use the PCI ASV API to retrieve a list of PCI ASV scans. 

For more information about PCI ASV, see [Get Started with PCI ASV Scanning](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/pci-asv/GetStarted.htm) in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

tags.name: Attestations
tags.description: After you submit a scan to Tenable for ASV review, you can use the Tenable PCI ASV API to retrieve a list of your attestations and see your current attestation request status. For more information, see [Create an Attestation](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/pci-asv/CreateAttestation.htm) in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

tags.name: PCI Scans
tags.description:
Tenable's PCI ASV streamlines the quarterly external vulnerability scan submission and dispute process as required by PCI 11.2.2. With pre-configured scan templates and an efficient evidence/dispute resolution process, Tenable (an Approved Scanning Vendor) can quickly prepare a compliant scan report for merchants and service providers. You can use the PCI ASV API to retrieve a list of PCI ASV scans. 

For more information about PCI ASV, see [Get Started with PCI ASV Scanning](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/pci-asv/GetStarted.htm) in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

get.description:
Returns a list of PCI attestations in PCI ASV. **Note:** You need to request access to this endpoint through Tenable support even if you have a valid PCI ASV license.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description:
Filters the list of PCI attestations by status. To retrieve attestations that are currently in progress but have yet to be submitted for review, you can use the following status types:
  • `IN_PROGRESS`—Attestations that are in progress.
  • `NEEDS_WORK`—Attestations that require additional work.
To retrieve attestations that are currently in review, you can use the following status types:
  • `IN_REVIEW`—Attestations that are currently under review.
  • `INFO_REQUESTED`—Attestations that require additional information for one or more disputes.
  • `INFO_PROVIDED`—Attestations where you have provided additional information but the information is yet to be reviewed.
To retrieve attestations that have completed the review process, you can use the following status types:
  • `FAILED`—Attestations that have failed review.
  • `PASSED`—Attestations that have passed review.
  • `CLOSED`—Attestations that are no longer active.
parameters.name: status_type

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `name:desc` would sort results by the `name` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `name:desc,started_at:asc` would first sort results by the `name` field in descending order and then by the `started_at` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable PCI ASV uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Returned if Tenable PCI ASV successfully retrieved the list of PCI attestations.

attestations.description: An array of PCI ASV attestation objects.

400.description: Returned if you do not have a license to use Tenable PCI ASV.

errors.message: Please contact Tenable support to enable API access for this endpoint in your container.

value.error: Please contact Tenable support to enable API access for this endpoint in your container.

403.description: Returned if you do not have the required permissions to view the list of attestations. For example, if you're an analyst user instead of an administrator.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

response.value:
429 Too Many Requests

nginx

500.description: Returned if an internal error occurred in Tenable PCI ASV.

value.message: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

get.description:
Returns the details for the specified PCI attestation. **Note:** You need to request access to this endpoint through Tenable support even if you have a valid PCI ASV license.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the PCI attestation you want to retrieve details for.

200.description: Returned if Tenable PCI ASV successfully retrieved the details for the specified PCI attestation.

400.description: Returned if you do not have a license to use Tenable PCI ASV.

get.description:
Returns a list of disputes for the specified PCI attestation. **Note:** You need to request access to this endpoint through Tenable support even if you have a valid PCI ASV license.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the PCI attestation you want to retrieve disputes for.
parameters.name: attestation_uuid

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `created_at:desc` would sort results by the `created_at` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `created_at:desc,severity:asc` would first sort results by the `created_at` field in descending order and then by the `severity` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable PCI ASV uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Returned if Tenable PCI ASV successfully retrieved the disputes for the specified PCI attestation.

disputes.description: An array of PCI ASV dispute objects.

400.description: Returned if you do not have a license to use Tenable PCI ASV.

Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the PCI attestation you want to retrieve undisputed failures for.
parameters.name: attestation_uuid

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `asset_name:desc` would sort results by the `asset_name` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `asset_name:desc,severity:asc` would first sort results by the `asset_name` field in descending order and then by the `severity` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable PCI ASV uses the default value of `0`.

get.description:
Returns a list of PCI assets attested in the specified attestation in PCI ASV. **Note:** You need to request access to this endpoint through Tenable support even if you have a valid PCI ASV license.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the PCI attestation you want to retrieve assets for.
parameters.name: attestation_uuid

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `fqdn:desc` would sort results by the `fqdn` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `fqdn:desc,scan_name:asc` would first sort results by the `fqdn` field in descending order and then by the `scan_name` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable PCI ASV uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Returned if Tenable PCI ASV successfully retrieved the list of assets for the PCI attestation.

assets.description: An array of PCI ASV attestation assets objects.

400.description: Returned if you do not have a license to use Tenable PCI ASV.

get.description:
Returns a list of PCI scans in PCI ASV. **Note:** You need to request access to this endpoint through Tenable support even if you have a valid PCI ASV license..
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `name:desc` would sort results by the `name` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `name:desc,started_at:asc` would first sort results by the `name` field in descending order and then by the `started_at` field in ascending order.

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve.

asset_count.description: The number of assets that were scanned.

updated_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan was last updated. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

name.description: The name of the PCI scan in PCI ASV.

started_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan was started. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`

failure_count.description: The number of PCI ASV failures.

cde.uuid.description: The UUID of the CDE in the Tenable PCI ASV.

import_status.description: The status of the scan in the PCI ASV workbench.

vulnerability_count.description: The total number of vulnerabilities detected in the PCI scan.

uuid.description: The UUID of the PCI scan in the Tenable PCI ASV.

ended_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan ended. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`

status.description: The progress of the scan ranging from 0 to 100.

latest_comment.description: The latest comment on the attestation.

submitted_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan was submitted for attestation. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

assessor.description: The username or email address of the assessor of the attestation.

dispute_count.description: The number of disputes for the attestation.

customer_uuid.description: The number of disputes for the attestation.

attestation_internal_notes_count.description: The number of internal notes for the attestation.

scan_completion_date.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan was completed. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

created_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the attestation was created. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

failure_count.description: The number of failures in the attestation.

created_by.description: The username or email address of the creator of the attestation.

uuid.description: The UUID of the attestation in Tenable PCI ASV.

asset_count.description: The number of assets in the attestation.

passed_dispute_count.description: The number of passed disputes in the attestation.

undisputed_failure_count.description: The number of undisputed failures in the attestation.

scan_expiration_date.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan will expire. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

updated_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the attestation was last updated. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

updated_by.description: The user that last updated the attestation.

name.description: The name of the PCI scan in PCI ASV.

failed_dispute_count.description: The number of failed disputes in the attestation.

cde.uuid.description: The UUID of the PCI scan in Tenable PCI ASV.

undetermined_dispute_count.description: The number of undetermined disputes in the attestation.

excluded_asset_count.description: The number of excluded assets in the attestation.

owner.description: The username or email address of the owner of the dispute.

plugin_id.description: The plugin ID in the PCI ASV scan for the dispute.

latest_comment.description: The latest comment on the dispute.

customer_uuid.description: The UUID of the customer in Tenable PCI ASV.

created_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the dispute was created. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

failure_count.description: The number of failures in the dispute.

plugin_name.example: PCI DSS Compliance : Remote Access Software Has Been Detected
plugin_name.description: The plugin name in the PCI ASV scan for the dispute.

created_by.description: The username or email address of the person that created the dispute.

determination_comment.description: A comment about the determination of the dispute.

uuid.description: The UUID of the attestation in PCI ASV.

cloned.description: Indicates whether the dispute is cloned or not.

updated_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the dispute was last updated. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

updated_by.description: The username or email address of the user that last updated the dispute.

scan_date.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan ended. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

Response-AttestationContact.description: The contact information for the owner of the attestation.

city.description: The city where the contact resides.

state.description: The state where the contact resides.

zip_code.description: The zip code for the contact's address.

country.description: The country where the contact resides.

submitted_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the attestation was submitted. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

submitted_by_username.description: The username of the person who submitted the attestation.

submitted_by_id.description: The UUID of the user that submitted the attestation.

dispute_failure_reason.description: The reason for the dispute failure.

dispute_failure_comments.description: Additional comments about the dispute failure.

excluded.description: The number of assets included or excluded

undisputed_failure_count.description: The number of undisputed failures in the attestation.

address.description: The IP address of the asset.

fqdn.description: The fully qualified domain name for the asset.

scan_uuid.description: The UUID of the scan associated with an asset.

operating_system.description: The operating system installed on the asset.

failure_count.description: The number of failures for the asset.

scan_name.description: The name of the scan associated with the asset.

uuid.description: The UUID of the PCI asset in the Tenable PCI ASV.

scan_date.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan ended. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`

plugin_id.description: The plugin ID in the PCI ASV scan for the dispute.

cvss_score.description: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score (intrinsic and fundamental characteristics of a vulnerability that are constant over time and user environments).

address.description: The IP address of the asset.

scan_uuid.description: The UUID of the scan associated with an asset.

duplicate.description: Indicates whether or not the failure is duplicated.

rerank_type.description: The rank type value of the undisputed failure.

plugin_name.description: The plugin name in the PCI ASV scan for the failure.

uuid.description: The UUID of the undisputed failure in PCI ASV.

protocol.description: The protocol value of the failure.

port.description: The port on which the plugin detected the failure.

scan_name.description: The name of the scan associated with the asset.

scan_date.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the scan ended. For example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`

Response-Pagination.description: An object containing pagination information for the current result set.

total.description: The total number of records matching your search criteria.

offset.description: The starting record you requested (or zero if omitted).

limit.description: The number of records requested (or the default value if omitted from the request).

sort.description: An array of objects specifying how the current result set is sorted.

Response-PaginationSortObject.description: An object specifying how the current result set is sorted, including the property by which the results were sorted and the sort direction.

name.description: The property used to sort the results.

order.description: The sort direction for the results. `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code of the error.

message.description: A brief message about the cause of the error.
